title: thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. improperum
intro: Thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. improperum, also known as the blue mountain rock cress or more commonly, poor man's pepper, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve flatulence, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. improperum include its potential to help reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with medications and allergies.
